funds.name: BNDES Fundo Clima – Indústria Verde
funds.description: Long-term loan that helps companies upgrade factories, swap inefficient boilers, install energy-efficiency measures, or develop bio-based inputs. Finances up to 80% of costs, with fixed concessional rates and a five-year grace period.
funds.prioritySectorsLabel: Industrial energy efficiency, bioeconomy, advanced biofuels, low-carbon industrial upgrades
funds.ticketWindowLabel: R$ 20m – R$ 500m per economic group / 12 months

funds.name: National Climate Fund (FNMC) – Grants
funds.description: Non-repayable funds from Brazil's Environment Ministry for cities, states, or NGOs to prepare climate plans, pilot adaptation measures, monitoring systems, or training programs. Requires at least a 5% local counterpart.
funds.eligibleBorrowersLabel: Municipal & state governments, public consortia, CSOs
funds.prioritySectorsLabel: Climate mitigation & adaptation studies, pilots, risk information systems
funds.ticketWindowLabel: R$ 2m – R$ 20m (set by each PAAR call)
funds.safeguards: Licences + municipal decree establishing project team

funds.name: IDB Specific Investment Loan (ESP)
funds.description: The IDB's flagship loan for public works: BRT corridors, wastewater plants, solar parks, resilient hospitals. Needs a federal guarantee and Senate approval. Up to 25 years to repay.
funds.eligibleBorrowersLabel: Federal, state or municipal governments (with sovereign guarantee)
funds.prioritySectorsLabel: Energy transition, resilient transport, water & sanitation, AFOLU, health, DRR
funds.financialCost: SOFR + 0.80% spread + 0.41% funding margin + 0.50% commitment fee

funds.name: CAF Environment & Climate-Change Credit Window
funds.description: Sovereign or sub-sovereign loan that can be blended with grants from the GEF or the Green Climate Fund to cut interest costs. Suits large low-carbon infrastructure or conservation projects with social co-benefits.
funds.eligibleBorrowersLabel: National & sub-national governments; PPP SPVs with public guarantee
funds.prioritySectorsLabel: Low-carbon infrastructure, biodiversity conservation, climate adaptation

funds.name: FONPLATA Green Line – Investment Loan
funds.description: Targets cities in the Plata Basin that need clean mobility, drainage, or urban green space. Typical tickets of US$ 20–60 million, 15-year terms, and streamlined safeguard rules.
funds.eligibleBorrowersLabel: Federal, state, municipal governments in Plata Basin
funds.prioritySectorsLabel: Urban mobility, water, flood control, green spaces, small hydro

funds.name: Pró-Cidades (Cities Program)
funds.description: Integrated urban development funding. Finances projects to improve urban public spaces, revitalize city centers, upgrade slums, and implement smart city solutions. Low-cost loans aimed at medium-sized cities.
funds.prioritySectorsLabel: Urban revitalization & smart cities – public space upgrades, social housing, lighting, urban governance tech
funds.safeguards: Align with municipal urban plans and federal urban development guidelines

funds.name: GCF Readiness Programme
funds.description: Grants for developing countries to strengthen institutional capacity, develop climate policies, and prepare project pipelines. Helps cities and national entities get ready for larger GCF investments.
funds.eligibleBorrowersLabel: National designated authorities and accredited entities
funds.prioritySectorsLabel: Institutional strengthening, climate policy development, project pipeline preparation

funds.name: C40 Cities Finance Facility
funds.description: Technical assistance to help cities develop bankable climate projects. Provides project preparation support including feasibility studies, financial structuring, and investor engagement.
funds.eligibleBorrowersLabel: C40 member cities and partner cities
funds.prioritySectorsLabel: All climate sectors – buildings, transport, waste, energy, adaptation
funds.ticketWindowLabel: In-kind support valued at up to US$ 1m

funds.name: Teia da Sociobiodiversidade
funds.description: Non-reimbursable funding for community-based organizations working on sociobiodiversity value chains — riverine, quilombola, Indigenous and family-farming groups. Supports productive use of nature-based solutions, seed networks, and community stewardship.
funds.prioritySectorsLabel: Sociobiodiversity value chains, community NBS, food security
funds.safeguards: Community approval + alignment with MDA/MMA social policy
funds.applicationChannel: Periodic public calls by MDA / MMA

funds.name: Fundo Casa Reconstruir RS
funds.description: Emergency and recovery grants for community organizations in Rio Grande do Sul affected by the 2024 floods. Funds nature-based recovery, community-led reconstruction, and climate resilience at neighborhood scale.
funds.eligibleBorrowersLabel: CSOs and CBOs operating in Rio Grande do Sul
funds.safeguards: Alignment with Fundo Casa social and environmental criteria

funds.name: GEF Small Grants Programme
funds.description: UNDP-administered small grants for community-based environmental action. Long-running global programme (active in Brazil) funding biodiversity, climate adaptation, land degradation, and international waters projects led by CBOs and Indigenous groups.
funds.eligibleBorrowersLabel: Community-based organizations, NGOs, Indigenous/traditional community groups
funds.prioritySectorsLabel: Biodiversity, climate, land, waters — community scale
funds.financingShare: Grant; in-kind or co-financing usually required
funds.safeguards: GEF SGP social and environmental standards
funds.applicationChannel: National GEF SGP country office (UNDP Brazil)

funds.name: Periferias Verdes Resilientes
funds.description: Federal programme supporting green infrastructure in urban peripheries. Combines direct CBO grants with municipal co-financing to accelerate nature-based interventions in under-served neighborhoods.
funds.eligibleBorrowersLabel: CBOs (direct) or in partnership with municipal governments
funds.prioritySectorsLabel: Urban NBS in peripheral neighborhoods, climate justice
funds.financingShare: Grant; may require municipal partnership agreement
funds.safeguards: Federal programme safeguards + community benefit plan

funds.name: Petrobras Socioambiental — NBS Urbano
funds.description: Corporate sustainability programme funding nature-based solutions in Brazilian cities. Typically accessed via consortium: CBOs partner with a municipality or a larger anchor organization to apply.
funds.eligibleBorrowersLabel: CBOs in consortium with municipalities or anchor NGOs
funds.prioritySectorsLabel: Urban NBS, climate adaptation, community stewardship
funds.safeguards: Petrobras social programme criteria + environmental plan

preparation_facility.name: Project Preparation Facility
preparation_facility.description: Project Preparation Facilities provide grants or technical assistance to help cities develop their project ideas into bankable proposals. They fund feasibility studies, cost estimates, environmental assessments, and financial structuring.
preparation_facility.whenToUse: Your project is at an early stage (idea or concept only), you lack feasibility studies or cost estimates, or you need help making your project attractive to investors.
preparation_facility.nextSteps: Once your project is prepared with proper studies and documentation, you can approach larger funds for implementation financing.

grant.name: Grant-Only / Public-Good Fund
grant.description: Grant funds provide non-repayable financing for projects that generate public benefits but lack commercial returns. They typically support adaptation, nature-based solutions, capacity building, and pilot projects.
grant.whenToUse: Your project delivers public benefits (climate resilience, ecosystem services) but won't generate revenue to repay a loan. Common for adaptation and nature-based solutions.
grant.nextSteps: Focus on demonstrating measurable climate and social benefits. Document lessons learned to inform future projects that might attract blended finance.

domestic_bank.name: Domestic / Regional Development Bank
domestic_bank.description: Domestic development banks offer concessional loans with lower interest rates and longer terms than commercial banks. They understand local context and regulations, making them accessible for well-prepared municipal projects.
domestic_bank.whenToUse: Your project has completed feasibility, can demonstrate a clear repayment source, and your city can legally borrow. Projects typically need to align with national development priorities.
domestic_bank.nextSteps: Prepare a strong project proposal with clear financial projections. Engage early with the bank to understand specific requirements and application timelines.

multilateral.name: Multilateral Development Bank
multilateral.description: Multilateral banks like IDB, CAF, and World Bank provide large-scale financing for transformative projects. They require sovereign guarantees and rigorous preparation but offer competitive terms and technical support.
multilateral.whenToUse: Your project is large-scale (typically >US$20m), has advanced preparation, and can obtain national government support and sovereign guarantee.
multilateral.nextSteps: Engage national government early for sovereign guarantee. Prepare comprehensive feasibility studies and safeguard documents.

aggregation.name: Aggregation / Programmatic Pathway
aggregation.description: When individual projects are too small to access large funds, cities can bundle multiple projects together. This creates economies of scale and spreads transaction costs across a larger investment.
aggregation.whenToUse: Your project is smaller than fund minimum thresholds, the project type is replicable across locations, and you're open to coordinating with other cities or sites.
aggregation.nextSteps: Connect with regional or national programs that aggregate similar projects. Consider city networks or consortia that can pool multiple projects.
